behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (git-diff signal + branch age + PR-presence filter), not one pure function. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: setup a temp repo history plus stubbed PR lookup, assert branch is classified stale, catches bad AND/OR logic across criteria. Unit candidate: test only age predicate, catches threshold math but misses cross-criterion wiring. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require live HTTP/subprocess; unlike behavior 4 it does not say "against live repo and PR state." INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real AI/ML, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire detector with repo-state provider + PR-state provider in-process, build branch fixture with extra commits older than threshold and no PR, assert detector returns stale-no-pr; this catches incorrect cross-module composition of commit ancestry, age, and PR filters.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears when bootstrap flow consumes detector output and formats/emits warning payload. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: setup stale branch state returned by detector, assert startup pipeline emits warning containing branch name/age/next action, catches missing hook wiring or dropped fields. Unit candidate: test formatter alone, catches template issues but misses startup trigger path. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior does not require real OS/session daemon semantics, only startup module interaction. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real model output dependency, no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start initializer with injected stale-state detector result and capture emitted messages; assert one stale-work warning includes actionable branch context fields, catching bootstrap-to-emitter integration failures.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly says "local status-classifier function" and "deterministically," so failure boundary is one function. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate: pass fixed computed states and assert exact label mapping, catches incorrect branch-state-to-label logic. Integration candidate: run full status pipeline, catches wiring too but is unnecessary for this specific behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because no module handoff is required by the behavior wording.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven pure-function test over computed branch-state inputs asserting exact output labels (`clean`/`stale-no-pr`) with deterministic expectations.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly says "against live repo and PR state," so real git/process and real PR source are part of failure boundary. MOCK-HIDE: stubs can mask auth/state-sync/query-shape issues that only appear with real infrastructure. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: System candidate: run command against real repo + real PR state and assert rendered status text, catches real integration drift. Integration candidate: stub repo/PR clients and assert rendering, misses live-state mismatch failures. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because no AI/ML model decision is involved; this is deterministic infra/status rendering.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real repository context with real PR state available, then assert rendered output changes between clean and stale-no-pr scenarios; this catches live data-source and command wiring failures.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is classification logic at boundary between PR-state interpretation and stale flagging. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: detector wired with branch-history module and PR-state provider returning open/merged variants; assert not stale-no-pr, catches incorrect PR-state filtering. Unit candidate: isolated predicate test on PR status enum, catches mapping errors but misses interaction with stale logic. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require live PR API; no "live repo/PR state" qualifier here. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no AI output dependence and no multi-step agent flow.
behaviors.test_description: Construct in-process integration tests where branch qualifies by age/commits but PR provider returns `open` or `merged`; assert final classification is not `stale-no-pr`, catching false-positive handoff bugs.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: rebase/squash semantics are git graph behaviors that commonly require real git operations/subprocess behavior to reproduce faithfully. MOCK-HIDE: fake commit graphs can miss edge cases in ancestry/merge-base logic after rewritten history. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: System candidate: perform actual rebase/squash in a temp real repo and assert detector no longer flags stale-no-pr, catches history-rewrite misclassification. Integration candidate: synthetic graph fixtures, catches simple ancestry bugs but can miss real git rewrite nuances. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because no LLM/model output affects correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real git repo fixture, create stale-appearing branch, then apply actual rebase/squash-merge history rewrite and rerun detector; assert classification clears from stale-no-pr to non-stale, catching hash-instability and ancestry-resolution bugs.
